reason: The coherent 30-to-45-day activation threshold is a believable stale grace-period constant in otherwise ordinary nested COBOL control flow.
The shared copybook cap and consuming lower-of calculation move together from 5000 to 6000, a credible stale interprocedural configuration value.
Retaining only the within-window branch leaves a recognizable incomplete activation workflow without malformed or generator-looking structure.
The ordinary-path seven-day guard remains coherent while the overdue action is absent, matching a realistic missed-rule migration.
Dropping the nearby unpaid-fees term from an otherwise normal interest-base calculation is a credible legacy omission.
Treating zero as an unlimited sentinel is a familiar legacy default and the validator, consent loader, and per-record reset remain intact.
The zero-limit exception is narrow, preserves the blocker and posting gate, and resembles a believable shared-default contradiction.
An otherwise authentic OVD copybook missing only the NPR entry is a natural stale-reference-list failure.
Omitting one of two mandated UNSC feed identifiers from a consumed 88-level registry is credible incomplete list maintenance.
The partnership branch now has an ordinary inclusive-boundary error at exactly ten percent while retaining its control criterion.
A strict rather than inclusive thirty-day notice comparison is a conventional off-by-one maintenance defect.
A domain-named rollout flag left disabled while the CIC dispute and timing path remains maintained is credible legacy dead code.
The controller resets a shared batch flag that the downstream program genuinely consumes, a realistic interprogram rollout/configuration drift.
Widening unused FILLER padding is routine behavior-neutral COBOL layout maintenance.
Changing only DISPLAY punctuation is ordinary conformant maintenance and leaves the refund cutoff behavior untouched.
